name: sweetbean

metadata.name: sweetbean
metadata.summary: A declarative language in python for creating jsPsych experiments

documentation: # sweetBean A declarative language in python for creating jsPsych experiments. This is a partner product intended to use alongside sweetPea (https://github.com/sweetpea-org/sweetpea-py) but can also be used as standalone product. This project is in active development by the Autonomous Empirical Research Group (package developer: Younes Strittmatter, PI: Sebastian Musslick). This research program was supported by Schmidt Science Fellows, in partnership with the Rhodes Trust, as well as the Carney BRAINSTORM program at Brown University. # install ``` pip install sweetbean ``` # usage: This is a very early version. You can find examples and documentation here: https://autoresearch.github.io/sweetbean/

classes.name: sweetbean.stimulus.Video.Video

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus.Video.Video.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    duration: time in ms the stimulus is presented
    src: the path to the image
    choices: the keys that will be recorded if pressed
    correct_key: the correct key to press
shows a video

classes.name: sweetbean.stimulus.RO.RandomObjectKinematogram

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus.RO.RandomObjectKinematogram.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    duration: time in ms the stimulus is presented // trial_duration
    number_of_oobs: the number of oriented objects per set in the stimulus
    number_of_apertures: the number of kinematograms
    coherent_movement_direction: the direction of coherent motion in degrees
        (0 degre meaning right)
    coherent_orientation: the orientation of the objects in degree
        (0 degree meaning right)
    coherence_movement: the percentage of oriented objects moving in the coherent direction.
    coherence_orientation: the percentage of oriented objects moving in the coherent
        direction.
    movement_speed: the movement speed of the oobs in (percentage of aperture_width)/second
    aperture_position_left: position of midpoint of aperture in x direction in percentage
        of window width
    aperture_position_top: position of midpoint of aperture in y direction in percentage
        of window height
    oob_color: the color of the orientated objects
    background_color: the background color
    choices: the valid keys that the subject can press to indicate a response
    correct_key: the correct key to press
Show a random-object-kinematogram

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus.RO.RandomDotPatterns.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    duration: time in ms the stimulus is presented // trial_duration
    number_of_oobs: the number of oriented objects per set in the stimulus
    number_of_apertures: the number of kinematograms
    coherent_orientation: the orientation of the objects in degree
        (0 degree meaning right)
    coherence_orientation: the percentage of oriented objects moving in the coherent
        direction.
    aperture_position_left: position of midpoint of aperture in x direction in percentage
        of window width
    aperture_position_top: position of midpoint of aperture in y direction in percentage
        of window height
    oob_color: the color of the orientated objects
    background_color: the background color
    choices: the valid keys that the subject can press to indicate a response
    correct_key: the correct key to press

classes.name: sweetbean.stimulus._Template_._Template_

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus._Template_._Template_.__init__
documentation: Change this to match the arguments of the class

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus._Template_._Template_.process_l
documentation:
This is used to process the arguments, generate a prompt and
get a response from language input

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus._Template_._Template_._add_special_param
documentation:
This can be used to add special parameters to the js object that are
not input arguments of the class

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus._Template_._Template_._process_response
documentation:
This can be used to process the responses given by the participant and
adding data to the js object

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus._Template_._Template_._set_before
documentation:
This can be used to set global parameters before the stimulus is presented
 (for example css variables)

A template for creating a new stimulus.
The following methods are probably sufficient to implement.

classes.name: sweetbean.stimulus.HtmlKeyboardResponse.Feedback

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus.HtmlKeyboardResponse.Feedback.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    duration: time in ms the stimulus is presented
    window: how far back is the stimulus to check
            (that stimulus needs to have a choice and a correct_key parameter)
show the word correct or incorrect dependent on a correct response to a stimulus before

classes.name: sweetbean.stimulus.HtmlKeyboardResponse.Flanker

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus.HtmlKeyboardResponse.Flanker.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    duration: time in ms the stimulus is presented
    direction: the direction of the target (allowed: left, right, l, r, L, R)
    distractor: the direction of the distractor (allowed: left, right, l, r, L, R)
    choices: the keys that will be recorded if pressed
    correct_key: the correct key to press
show a flanker stimulus (<< < <<; << > <<, >> < >>, ...)

classes.name: sweetbean.stimulus.HtmlKeyboardResponse.Symbol

methods.name: sweetbean.stimulus.HtmlKeyboardResponse.Symbol.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    duration: time in ms the stimulus is presented
    symbol: the symbol to show (allowed: square, triangle, circle)
    color: the color of the symbol
    choices: the keys that will be recorded if pressed
    correct_key: the correct key to press
show a symbol

classes.name: sweetbean.variable.TimelineVariable
documentation: A timeline variable for jsPsych.

classes.name: sweetbean.variable.DataVariable
documentation: A data variable for jsPsych to access data of previous trials.

classes.name: sweetbean.variable.FunctionVariable
documentation:
A variable that is the result of a function.
Examples:
    >>> def add(a, b):
    ...     return a + b
    >>> FunctionVariable('a', add, [1, 2]).to_js()
    ((a,b) => {return a+b})(1, 2)

classes.name: sweetbean.variable.CodeVariable
documentation: A variable to access JavaScript code.

classes.name: sweetbean.variable.SharedVariable
documentation: A variable that can be shared between different stimuli.

methods.name: sweetbean.variable.SideEffect.__init__
documentation: A side effect that can set variables.

functions.name: sweetbean.util.parse._fct_args_to_js
documentation:
Convert Python argument list to JavaScript inputs for a function.
Examples:

functions.name: sweetbean.util.parse._var_to_js
documentation: Convert a python input value to a JavaScript input value.

functions.name: sweetbean.util.parse._fct_to_js
documentation:
Convert a Python function to JavaScript using Transcrypt.

Examples:
\# >>> def add(a, b):
\# ... return a + b
\# >>> \_fct_to_js(add)
\# '(a,b) => {return a+b}'

```pycon
>>> a = lambda color: "f" if color == "red" else "j"
>>> _fct_to_js(a)
'(color) => {return color=="red"?"f":"j"}'
```

functions.name: sweetbean.util.parse._postprocess_format
documentation:
Replace `.format(...)` calls in the generated JavaScript
with equivalent JavaScript template literals.

functions.name: sweetbean.util.parse._postprocess_functions
documentation: Replace known Python functions with their JavaScript equivalents.
